model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Georgia holds a consistent edge in overall adjusted efficiency — KenPom #31 (AdjEM +20.47) and Torvik #37 versus Saint Louis's KenPom #41 (AdjEM +18.32) and Torvik #44 — driven primarily by a superior offense ranked 15th nationally (AdjOE 124.7) that is well-positioned to exploit a Saint Louis defense ranked 56th by Torvik, despite Georgia's own defensive liability (AdjDE 104.2, ranked 93rd nationally). The betting market's 2.5-point Georgia spread aligns with the efficiency gap and reflects the Bulldogs' SEC schedule-strength advantage over Saint Louis's A-10 résumé. Both teams enter with late-season form concerns — Georgia lost three straight to close the regular season and fell to Ole Miss in the SEC Tournament, while Saint Louis went 3-3 in their final six games and lost to Dayton in the A-10 semifinal — but Georgia's superior offensive firepower and tougher schedule validation make them the narrow right side in this genuinely competitive 8/9 coin-flip.

reasoningStep.title: Georgia vs. Saint Louis — Midwest 8/9 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Georgia holds a ~2-point AdjEM edge (KenPom) and a 7-spot Torvik advantage, with an elite offense ranked 15th nationally but a weak defense ranked 93rd by Torvik. Saint Louis counters with a better defense (56th by Torvik, AdjDE 101.2) and a 28-5 record, but their A-10 schedule is meaningfully weaker than Georgia's SEC slate. The betting market opened Georgia -2.5, consistent with the efficiency gap. Both teams have late-season form concerns. Georgia's offensive superiority and SEC battle-testing tip this classic coin-flip to the Bulldogs at low confidence.
evidence: Georgia: KenPom #31 AdjEM +20.47, AdjOE 124.7 (15th nationally), AdjDE 104.2 (93rd), Torvik #37. Saint Louis: KenPom #41 AdjEM +18.32, AdjOE 119.5 (51st), AdjDE 101.2 (56th), Torvik #44.
Georgia opened as a 2.5-point favorite over Saint Louis per Action Network, consistent with the efficiency gap.

Georgia is weak on the defensive glass, allowing nearly 35% offensive rebound rate, and lost to Ole Miss 76-72 in the SEC Tournament second round after dropping three straight to close the regular season.
Saint Louis went 3-3 in its last 6 games to close the regular season, finishing 28-5 overall and 15-3 in A-10 play, leading the conference in scoring at 87.3 PPG.
Saint Louis lost to Dayton in a wild finish in the A-10 Tournament semifinal, ending their conference tournament run early.
Georgia's SEC schedule-strength advantage over Saint Louis's A-10 résumé validates the efficiency gap and supports the Bulldogs as the narrow favorite in this 8/9 matchup.

Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Georgia vs. Saint Louis: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Georgia vs. Saint Louis. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Georgia vs. Saint Louis Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Georgia vs. Saint Louis Georgia 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mike White has done something remarkable in Athens. The Bulldogs have reached 20 wins for the third straight season, setting a program record with 22 regular-season victories, the most in 121 years of Georgia basketball. This team is explosive in ways that are genuinely entertaining to watch. Georgia leads the nation in dunks by percentage, Somto Cyril is the individual leader in dunks nationwide, and Georgia averages nearly 90 points per game, a program record. The offense runs through relentless rim attacks and guard creativity from Marcus Millender, Blue Cain, and leading scorer Jeremiah Wilkinson, with a pace that simply suffocates teams that can’t match their energy. Georgia’s late-season surge should be noted. The Bulldogs had dropped five of six games before rattling off wins over Kentucky, Texas, Alabama, South Carolina, and Mississippi State down the stretch to close at 10-8 in the SEC. The bubble sweats are over. The question now is ceiling. Georgia is 6-7 in Quad 1 and hasn’t beaten a true title contender this year. The Bulldogs rank higher in non-conference games than overall, but their non-con strength of schedule ranked outside the top-300. The defense is solid enough, with Cyril blocking shots at a top-20 rate nationally, but it’s not elite, and teams with real perimeter shooting can exploit their aggressiveness. Kanon Catchings can kick it from deep as a 39% perimeter shooter, but the Bulldogs rank outside the top 150 from deep, limiting them against slow-paced, pack-line defenses. In a first-round matchup, Georgia can be a real problem. Past that, it depends on wheth

result.text: Georgia vs. Saint Louis: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Georgia vs. Saint Louis: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Georgia vs. Saint Louis. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Georgia vs. Saint Louis Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Georgia vs. Saint Louis Georgia 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mike White has done something remarkable in Athens. The Bulldogs have reached 20 wins for the third straight season, setting a program record with 22 regular-season victories, the most in 121 years of Georgia basketball. This team is explosive in ways that are genuinely entertaining to watch. Georgia leads the nation in dunks by percentage, Somto Cyril is the individual leader in dunks nationwide, and Georgia averages nearly 90 points per game, a program record. The offense runs through relentless rim attacks and guard creativity from Marcus Millender, Blue Cain, and leading scorer Jeremiah Wilkinson, with a pace that simply suffocates teams that can’t match their energy. Georgia’s late-season surge should be noted. The Bulldogs had dropped five of six games before rattling off wins over Kentucky, Texas, Alabama, South Carolina, and Mississippi State down the stretch to close at 10-8 in the SEC. The bubble sweats are over. The question now is ceiling. Georgia is 6-7 in Quad 1 and hasn’t beaten a true title contender this year. The Bulldogs rank higher in non-conference games than overall, but their non-con strength of schedule ranked outside the top-300. The defense is solid enough, with Cyril blocking shots at a top-20 rate nationally, but it’s not elite, and teams with real perimeter shooting can exploit their aggressiveness. Kanon Catchings can kick it from deep as a 39% perimeter shooter, but the Bulldogs rank outside the top 150 from deep, limiting them against slow-paced, pack-line defenses. In a first-round matchup, Georgia can be a real problem. Past that, it depends on wheth

result.title: 2026 Atlantic 10 Tournament Previews: Saint Louis Billikens
result.snippet: SLU wrapped up one of the greatest regular seasons in program history, but the Billikens stumbled to the finish, going 3-3 in its last 6 games. Josh Schertz's team tore through the A-10 with a 27-3 record and 15-3 record in conference play, led by an explosive and diverse offensive attack that led the A-10 in scoring at 87.3 points per game.
